title: Neonauclea bartlingii
taxonomy: Neonauclea bartlingii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Neonauclea, which is a group of plants native to the tropical regions of Asia. The species is further classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. The classification of Neonauclea bartlingii is based on its morphological characteristics, including its leaf shape, flower structure, and growth habits. By classifying Neonauclea bartlingii within the Rubiaceae family, botanists can better understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.

intro: Neonauclea bartlingii, also known as the "Australian holly," is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce the severity of infections.
